description_long: This object is a round, green cabbage with tightly packed, overlapping leaves forming a dense, dome-shaped head. The leaves are arranged in a spiral rosette pattern, with each layer wrapping closely around the previous one, creating a textured, layered appearance. The surface displays various shades of green, with subtle gradients and natural veining visible on the leaf surfaces. The edges of the leaves are smooth and entire, with no visible serrations or lobes. There are no loose or separated outer leaves, and the central stem or core is not visible from any angle. The overall form is compact and solid, typical of a fresh cabbage head found in markets or urban gardens.
description: A round, green cabbage with tightly layered, overlapping leaves in a spiral rosette pattern and a compact, dome-shaped form.
description_view_0: View 0 shows the cabbage from a slightly angled side, highlighting the layered, spiral arrangement of the leaves and the smooth, entire leaf margins.
description_view_1: View 1 presents a similar side perspective, emphasizing the dense, overlapping leaf structure and the subtle color gradients across the surface.
description_view_2: View 2 offers another side view, with the spiral leaf pattern and compactness of the head clearly visible, showing no loose outer leaves.
description_view_3: View 3 displays the cabbage from a slightly different angle, again revealing the tight, layered leaf arrangement and the uniform green coloration.
